steps.title: Identify the scope clue in the notes
steps.content: The notes reveal that Hopkins “created many genre-defining stories” during her career. This describes her scope and significance—exactly what's needed when introducing someone to an unfamiliar audience.

steps.title: Match the answer that incorporates this clue
steps.content: Choice B is the only option that uses the “many genre-defining stories” detail, framing Hagar's Daughter as an example of her broader output. This comprehensive picture gives an unfamiliar audience a proper introduction to her career. The other choices focus narrowly on that single novel or publication logistics without conveying her broader legacy. That matches choice B — B is correct.
